quick_facts.label: Award and provider
quick_facts.value: NCHMCT offers this full-time B.Sc. programme through its affiliated Institutes of Hotel Management; NCHMCT states that successful graduates are awarded a JNU degree.

quick_facts.label: Standard duration
quick_facts.value: Six semesters (three years), with an optional fourth year under the NEP 2020 framework for an Honours degree, subject to completing the extended curriculum.

quick_facts.label: Entry qualification
quick_facts.value: Pass the 10+2 Senior Secondary examination or equivalent from a recognised board/institution, with English as a subject. There is no age bar stated for the programme.

quick_facts.label: Main admission route
quick_facts.value: For the NCHMCT affiliated route, admission is based on All India Rank in NCHM JEE, followed by centralised online counselling and institute/seat allotment on merit-cum-choice.

quick_facts.label: 2026 fee context
quick_facts.value: NCHMCT's 2026 brochure lists institute-variable tuition of approximately ₹3.00 lakh–₹5.00 lakh for the six-semester programme, in addition to listed common fees such as admission, enrolment, JNU, examination, and refundable academic caution-money charges. Fees should be confirmed with the allotted institute before payment.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: For the NCHMCT-affiliated B.Sc. in Hospitality and Hotel Administration, applicants must have passed the 10+2 Senior Secondary examination (or an equivalent recognised qualification) with English as one of the subjects. Candidates awaiting their qualifying result may apply provisionally, but must submit proof of passing by admission or by the deadline specified by NCHMCT; the current programme page states 30 September of the admission year. NCHMCT states that there is no age bar for this programme.
bullets: English may be studied as a core, elective, or functional subject in the qualifying examination.
Always check the applicable admission bulletin and counselling notice for the relevant academic session, as operational rules can change.

sections.heading: Duration and delivery
paragraphs: The standard programme is a full-time, six-semester B.Sc. spanning three years. NCHMCT also describes an optional fourth year aligned with NEP 2020 that can lead to an Honours degree on successful completion of the extended curriculum. The programme is delivered through NCHMCT's network of affiliated IHMs, which includes central-government, state-government, PSU-run, and private-sector institutes.
bullets: Extended option: an optional fourth year for an Honours degree, where offered and completed under the prescribed curriculum.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: The curriculum combines operational laboratory training with hospitality-management learning. NCHMCT identifies the core operational areas as Food Production, Food and Beverage Service, Front Office Operations, and Housekeeping. It also lists management-oriented subjects including Hotel Accountancy, Food Safety and Quality, Human Resource Management, Facility Planning, Revenue/Financial Management, Strategic Management, Tourism Marketing, and Tourism Management.
bullets: Practical operational learning is a central part of the degree, rather than an add-on to classroom study.
The published academic structure includes a 17-week industrial-training block in Semester III or Semester IV, depending on the student group/calendar arrangement.
The degree is designed around preparation for supervisory responsibilities in hospitality, with progression toward managerial roles.

sections.heading: Admission process
paragraphs: The principal national admission route for NCHMCT-affiliated B.Sc. HHA seats is NCHM JEE, conducted by the National Testing Agency in computer-based-test mode. NCHMCT states that candidates are admitted according to their NCHM JEE All India Rank, then participate in centralised online counselling, register their preferences, and receive institute/seat allotment on a merit-cum-choice basis.
bullets: Prepare for the NCHM JEE subject areas published in the 2026 brochure: numerical ability and analytical aptitude, reasoning and logical deduction, general knowledge and current affairs, English language, and aptitude for the service sector.

Application dates, examination dates, participating institutes, seat availability, reservation procedures, and counselling rounds are session-specific. Verify them through the current NTA and NCHMCT notices rather than relying on a prior-year schedule.

sections.heading: Costs and financial planning
paragraphs: Costs vary materially by institute. In NCHMCT's 2026 admission brochure, the stated tuition range is approximately ₹3.00 lakh to ₹5.00 lakh for the whole six-semester programme. The same source separately lists common charges: ₹1,600 admission fee, ₹3,600 NCHMCT enrolment fee, ₹10,215 JNU fee, ₹3,000 examination fee per semester, and ₹8,000 refundable academic caution money. These figures are dated 2026 context, not a universal quote for every institute or later intake.
bullets: Request the institute's current fee notice and ask whether tuition covers uniforms, tools, laboratory charges, training-related expenses, accommodation, meals, and transport.
NCHMCT's 2026 brochure points students to national scholarship schemes and notes that state-level support may also be available, subject to each scheme's eligibility and current rules.

sections.heading: Progression and career options
paragraphs: Graduates can progress into hospitality operations and supervisory-track roles, including hotel management trainee, guest/customer-relations, sales and marketing, catering, railway hospitality, retail, cruise/shipping, and tourism-related work. Actual job title, employer requirements, placement outcomes, and pay vary by location, employer, prior practical experience, and the individual institute's placement process.
bullets: A direct academic progression is the two-year M.Sc. in Hospitality Administration, jointly offered by NCHMCT and IGNOU at select IHMs; its Semester III includes a choice between Human Resource Management and Sales and Marketing specialisation.
Another related postgraduate option is IGNOU's Master of Tourism and Travel Management, whose published eligibility includes B.Sc. Hospitality and Hotel Administration graduates.

Entrepreneurial routes can include restaurants, catering, bakery/patisserie, food-service ventures, and other hospitality services; business viability depends on licensing, capital, local demand, and operating capability.
